# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security


// Next.js API route support with [req, res] arguments ✨️🔥 by Vercel's Edge Functions (Next.js) 🚀!
export default function handler(req, res){   // req is the incoming request and contains headers & body info in JSON format; response can be sent immediately or you could use a callback to send it later ✨️🔥 by Vercel's Edge Functions (Next.js) 🚀!
    const authCookieName = 'auth-token'; // Name of the cookie used for authentication, this should match with your server setup if you are using JWT or similar token based approach ✨️🔥 by Vercel's Edge Functions (Next.js) 🚀!
    const authCookie = req.cookies[authCookieName]; // Get the authentication cookie from request, if it exists in cookies else return unauthenticated response ✨️🔥 by Vercel's Edge Functions (Next.js) 🚀!
    
    const isAuthenticated = authCookie ? true : false; // Check the authentication status from cookie, if it exists in cookies else return unauthenticated response ✨️🔥 by Vercel's Edge Functions (Next.js) 🚀!  
    
    res.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Credentials', 'true'); // Set CORS headers to allow credentials in cookies, this is necessary for secure authentication ✨️🔥 by Vercel's Edge Functions (Next.js) 🚀!  
    
    if(isAuthenticated){// If user authenticate then proceed with request else deny the access and return unauthorized response or redirect to login page ✨️🔥 by Vercel's Edge Functions (Next.js) 🚀!  
        // Proceed further if authentication is successful, otherwise handle error accordingly in your application logic    } else { res.status(401).json({message: 'Unauthorized'});} ✨️🔥 by Vercel's Edge Functions (Next.js) 🚀!
